intent: Check whether the candidate can distinguish route parameters, query string parameters, and parsed request body data, and choose the correct place for each kind of API input.
primaryQuestion: How are req.params, req.query, and req.body different in Express? When should you use each one?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that req.params contains named path parameters declared in the route pattern, such as /users/:id.
Explains that req.query contains query string values after the question mark, usually used for optional filters, pagination, sorting, search, or feature flags.
Explains that req.body contains parsed request payload data, usually used for create/update commands such as POST, PUT, or PATCH.
Mentions that req.body is undefined by default unless body-parsing middleware such as express.json() or express.urlencoded() runs before the route.
Treats req.params, req.query, and req.body as user-controlled input that must be validated, normalized, and converted to the expected types.
Uses a concrete example such as GET /users/:id?includePosts=true using params and query, versus POST /users using body.

redFlags: Says req.params, req.query, and req.body are interchangeable.
Uses req.body for GET filters or req.query for required resource identity without explaining the API design trade-off.
Assumes values from req.query or req.params already have the correct numeric or boolean type.
Forgets that body parsers are required before req.body is populated.
Trusts any of these values without validation.

scoringHints.description: Clearly separates path identity, URL options, and request payload; gives examples; mentions parser middleware, type conversion, and validation.
Correctly describes the three request sources but misses one nuance such as body parser order or untrusted input.
Can define one or two properties but cannot choose consistently in practical REST endpoint design.
Answer shows confusion about where Express stores request data.

followUps.question: For GET /products/:category?page=2 and POST /products, which values would you read from params, query, and body?
followUps.purpose: Force a concrete mapping between URL structure and request properties.

followUps.question: Why might req.query.limit be a string even when the client sends ?limit=10?
followUps.purpose: Check type conversion and validation awareness.

en.title: req.params vs req.query vs req.body
en.displayQuestion: How are req.params, req.query, and req.body different in Express? When should you use each one?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can choose the correct request data source for route identity, URL options, and request payloads.
guidance: Use route parameters for required resource identity in the path.
Use query string values for optional modifiers such as filter, pagination, sort, and search.
Use body data for submitted payloads in create or update operations.
Mention parser middleware, type conversion, and validation for all user-controlled input.
commonMistakes: Treating params, query, and body as interchangeable.
Forgetting that req.body requires parser middleware.
Assuming query values are already numbers or booleans.
Trusting parsed input without validation.

vi.title: req.params, req.query và req.body
vi.displayQuestion: req.params, req.query, req.body khác nhau như thế nào? Khi nào dùng cái nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có chọn đúng nguồn dữ liệu request cho định danh route, option trên URL và payload hay không.
guidance: Dùng route parameter cho định danh tài nguyên bắt buộc nằm trong path.
Dùng query string cho các tùy chọn như filter, pagination, sort và search.
Dùng body cho dữ liệu client gửi lên khi tạo hoặc cập nhật tài nguyên.
Nhắc tới body parser, chuyển kiểu dữ liệu và validation vì tất cả đều là input do client kiểm soát.
commonMistakes: Xem params, query và body là thay thế được cho nhau.
Quên req.body cần parser middleware.
Tưởng query value đã là number hoặc boolean.
Tin dữ liệu đã parse mà không validate.

ja.title: req.params、req.query、req.body
ja.displayQuestion: Express で req.params、req.query、req.body はどう違い、いつ使い分けますか。
ja.displayIntent: route identity、URL options、request payload に対して正しい request data source を選べるか確認する。
guidance: path 内の必須 resource identity には route parameter を使う。
filter、pagination、sort、search などの任意条件には query string を使う。
作成や更新で送られる payload には body data を使う。
body parser、type conversion、user-controlled input の validation に触れる。
commonMistakes: params、query、body を同じものとして扱う。
req.body に parser middleware が必要な点を忘れる。
query value が最初から number や boolean だと考える。
parse 済み input を validation せず信頼する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.params
sourceReferences.note: Used for route parameter behavior and automatic decoding.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.query
sourceReferences.note: Used for query parser behavior and untrusted query input guidance.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.body
sourceReferences.note: Used for request body parser requirements and untrusted body input guidance.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands HTTP request headers, how Express exposes them, and when to prefer req.get() over directly indexing req.headers.
primaryQuestion: What is req.headers, and how do you read a specific header in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Defines req.headers as the incoming HTTP request headers object inherited from Node.js IncomingMessage.
Uses req.get('Header-Name') or req.header('Header-Name') to read one header with case-insensitive matching.
Mentions that direct access through req.headers uses lower-case header names in Node.js, such as req.headers['authorization'].
Explains common use cases such as Authorization, Content-Type, Accept-Language, X-Request-Id, and custom client headers.
Treats client-supplied headers as untrusted and avoids trusting spoofable headers such as X-Forwarded-For unless proxy trust is configured correctly.
Handles missing headers explicitly instead of assuming a header is always present.

redFlags: Thinks req.headers only contains custom headers or only response headers.
Reads headers with case-sensitive direct property names such as req.headers.Authorization.
Trusts Authorization or X-Forwarded-* headers without validation or proxy context.
Crashes when a header is missing.
Confuses request headers with response headers set by res.set().

scoringHints.description: Explains Node/Express header storage, uses req.get(), handles case-insensitivity and missing values, and recognizes trust/security concerns.
Can read a specific header correctly but misses direct req.headers casing or trust implications.
Knows headers exist but is inconsistent about request versus response direction or case handling.
Answer does not demonstrate practical header access in Express.

followUps.question: How would you read the Authorization header, and what would you do if it is missing?
followUps.purpose: Check practical access and error handling.

followUps.question: Why is req.headers.Authorization usually not the safest direct property access pattern in Node.js?

en.title: Reading request headers
en.displayQuestion: What is req.headers, and how do you read a specific header in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check HTTP request header fundamentals and practical Express access patterns.
guidance: Define request headers as client metadata sent with the request.
Prefer req.get() or req.header() for a single header.
Mention Node's lower-case req.headers keys when accessing directly.
Explain missing-header handling and trust boundaries.
commonMistakes: Confusing request headers with response headers.
Using case-sensitive direct access incorrectly.
Trusting spoofable headers without proxy context.
Assuming headers are always present.

vi.title: Đọc request header
vi.displayQuestion: req.headers là gì và làm thế nào để đọc một header cụ thể?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng HTTP request header và cách đọc header thực tế trong Express.
guidance: Định nghĩa request header là metadata client gửi kèm request.
Ưu tiên req.get() hoặc req.header() khi đọc một header cụ thể.
Nhắc rằng key trong req.headers của Node thường được normalize về chữ thường.
Giải thích cách xử lý header bị thiếu và ranh giới tin cậy.
commonMistakes: Nhầm request header với response header.
Dùng direct access sai vì phân biệt hoa thường.
Tin header có thể bị spoof mà không xét proxy context.
Giả định header luôn tồn tại.

ja.title: request header を読む
ja.displayQuestion: req.headers とは何で、Express で特定の header をどう読みますか。
ja.displayIntent: HTTP request header の基礎と Express での実用的な access pattern を確認する。
guidance: request header は client が request に付ける metadata だと説明する。
単一 header には req.get() または req.header() を使う。
Node の req.headers の key は通常 lower-case になる点に触れる。
missing header の扱いと trust boundary を説明する。
commonMistakes: request header と response header を混同する。
case-sensitive な direct access を誤って使う。
proxy context なしに spoof 可能な header を信頼する。
header が常に存在すると仮定する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.get()
sourceReferences.note: Used for case-insensitive request header lookup and req.header alias.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js HTTP API: message.headers
sourceReferences.note: Used for the underlying IncomingMessage headers object.

intent: Check whether the candidate can correctly configure body parsing middleware, understand Content-Type matching, and validate parsed body input.
primaryQuestion: How do you read data from the request body in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Registers body-parsing middleware before routes that need req.body, commonly app.use(express.json()) for JSON payloads.
Uses express.urlencoded({ extended: true or false }) for application/x-www-form-urlencoded form submissions when needed.
Reads parsed values from req.body inside route handlers after the parser has run.
Mentions Content-Type matching: a JSON parser only parses requests whose content type matches the configured type.
Configures body size limits appropriate to the endpoint to avoid accepting unexpectedly large payloads.
Validates req.body because parsing does not guarantee correct schema, types, required fields, or safe values.
Mentions special cases such as raw body verification for webhooks or multipart/form-data requiring different middleware.

redFlags: Reads req.body without registering any parser and cannot explain why it is undefined.
Registers express.json() after the routes that need it.
Uses JSON parsing for multipart file uploads without explaining multer or another multipart parser.
Trusts parsed body data without validation.
Leaves public endpoints with overly broad or unlimited body size limits.

scoringHints.description: Covers middleware order, parser choice, Content-Type matching, limits, validation, and special body formats.
Knows express.json() and req.body but misses one production detail such as limits, content type, or validation.
Can read body values only in a simple happy path and cannot debug undefined or malformed body cases.
Answer does not demonstrate practical request body handling in Express.

followUps.question: Why would req.body be undefined even though the client sent JSON?
followUps.purpose: Probe middleware order and Content-Type reasoning.

followUps.question: When would express.raw() be more appropriate than express.json()?
followUps.purpose: Check body parser choice for webhook signature verification and binary payloads.

en.title: Reading request body data
en.displayQuestion: How do you read data from the request body in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can configure body parsers and safely consume parsed payloads.
guidance: Register express.json() or express.urlencoded() before routes.
Choose the parser based on Content-Type and payload format.
Read values from req.body after middleware runs.
Discuss size limits, validation, and special cases like webhooks or multipart uploads.
commonMistakes: Forgetting parser middleware.
Registering the parser after the route.
Using the wrong parser for multipart data.
Trusting req.body without schema validation.

vi.title: Đọc dữ liệu request body
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để đọc dữ liệu từ request body trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có cấu hình body parser đúng và xử lý payload đã parse một cách an toàn hay không.
guidance: Đăng ký express.json() hoặc express.urlencoded() trước route.
Chọn parser theo Content-Type và định dạng payload.
Đọc dữ liệu từ req.body sau khi middleware đã chạy.
Nói về size limit, validation và các trường hợp đặc biệt như webhook hoặc multipart upload.
commonMistakes: Quên parser middleware.
Đăng ký parser sau route.
Dùng sai parser cho multipart data.
Tin req.body mà không validate schema.

ja.title: request body data を読む
ja.displayQuestion: Express で request body の data をどう読みますか。
ja.displayIntent: body parser を設定し、parse 済み payload を安全に扱えるか確認する。
guidance: route より前に express.json() または express.urlencoded() を登録する。
Content-Type と payload format に応じて parser を選ぶ。
middleware 実行後に req.body から値を読む。
size limit、validation、webhook や multipart upload などの特殊ケースに触れる。
commonMistakes: parser middleware を忘れる。
route の後に parser を登録する。
multipart data に誤った parser を使う。
schema validation なしに req.body を信頼する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: express.json()
sourceReferences.note: Used for JSON parser behavior, Content-Type matching, limits, and untrusted input guidance.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.body
sourceReferences.note: Used for req.body default behavior and parser middleware examples.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands common Express response-ending methods and can choose between automatic Express helpers and low-level response termination.
primaryQuestion: How are res.send(), res.json(), and res.end() different in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that res.send() sends a response body and performs Express conveniences such as setting Content-Type or Content-Length for common body types.
Explains that res.json() serializes a value with JSON.stringify semantics and sets the JSON content type.
Explains that res.end() is the lower-level Node response termination method and does not perform the same Express body serialization conveniences.
Mentions that all three end the response cycle, so route code should not try to send another response afterward.
Chooses res.json() for API JSON payloads, res.send() for simple strings/HTML/Buffers or generic bodies, and res.end() for cases with no body or after manual streaming/writing.
Mentions that status and headers should be set before the response is ended.

redFlags: Thinks res.json() and res.send() always behave identically.
Uses res.end(object) expecting Express to serialize it to JSON.
Attempts to call res.json() after res.end() or res.send().
Does not understand that sending a response ends the request-response cycle.
Sets headers after the body has already been sent.

scoringHints.description: Contrasts serialization, content type behavior, response lifecycle, and appropriate use cases for all three methods.
Understands json versus send but gives shallow or incomplete explanation of end and lifecycle.
Knows the method names but cannot explain when to choose each one safely.
Answer risks duplicate responses or incorrect response body handling.

followUps.question: What would happen if you call res.end({ ok: true }) compared with res.json({ ok: true })?
followUps.purpose: Check serialization and low-level response understanding.

followUps.question: Can you set another header after res.send() has already sent the response? Why?
followUps.purpose: Probe response lifecycle and headers-sent reasoning.

en.title: res.send, res.json, and res.end
en.displayQuestion: How are res.send(), res.json(), and res.end() different in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check response helper selection and response lifecycle fundamentals.
guidance: Explain what each method sends and whether it serializes the body.
Mention Content-Type behavior and Express conveniences.
State that each method ends the response lifecycle.
Describe when status and headers must be set.
commonMistakes: Calling res.end() with an object and expecting JSON.
Sending multiple responses from one handler.
Setting headers after sending the body.
Using res.send() for JSON APIs without understanding response shape.

vi.title: res.send, res.json và res.end
vi.displayQuestion: res.send(), res.json(), res.end() khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng chọn response helper đúng và hiểu vòng đời response.
guidance: Giải thích mỗi method gửi gì và có serialize body hay không.
Nhắc tới Content-Type và các tiện ích Express tự xử lý.
Nói rõ mỗi method đều kết thúc response lifecycle.
Mô tả status và header phải được set trước khi gửi response.
commonMistakes: Gọi res.end() với object rồi kỳ vọng thành JSON.
Gửi nhiều response trong một handler.
Set header sau khi body đã gửi.
Dùng res.send() cho JSON API mà không hiểu response shape.

ja.title: res.send、res.json、res.end
ja.displayQuestion: Express の res.send()、res.json()、res.end() はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: response helper の選択と response lifecycle の基礎を確認する。
guidance: 各 method が何を送信し、body を serialize するか説明する。
Content-Type behavior と Express の convenience に触れる。
各 method が response lifecycle を終了することを述べる。
status と header は送信前に設定する必要があると説明する。
commonMistakes: object を res.end() に渡して JSON になると期待する。
1 つの handler から複数 response を送る。
body 送信後に header を設定する。
response shape を理解せず JSON API で res.send() を使う。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.send()
sourceReferences.note: Used for send behavior and automatic response conveniences.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.json()
sourceReferences.note: Used for JSON serialization and content type behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js HTTP API: response.end()
sourceReferences.note: Used for low-level response termination semantics behind Express.

intent: Check whether the candidate can set HTTP response status codes correctly and choose meaningful status codes for common API outcomes.
primaryQuestion: How do you set the HTTP status code in an Express response?

expectedSignals.label: Uses res.status(code) before sending the response body, such as res.status(201).json(createdResource).
Mentions res.sendStatus(code) as a shortcut that sets the status and sends the standard status message body.
Understands that status must be set before res.send(), res.json(), res.end(), res.redirect(), or file sending has committed headers.
Chooses common API statuses correctly, such as 200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, and 500 depending on the case.
Avoids using 200 for errors or 500 for every client-side problem.
Mentions valid HTTP status range expectations and that invalid codes may throw at the Node layer.

redFlags: Calls res.json(...).status(404) after the response was already sent.
Always returns 200 with an error field in the body.
Uses 500 for validation failures, not-found resources, or authorization failures.
Does not distinguish res.status() from res.sendStatus().
Sets statusCode directly in inconsistent places without understanding when headers are sent.

scoringHints.description: Uses res.status before sending, distinguishes sendStatus, selects appropriate status codes, and understands response commit timing.
Knows res.status(code) but misses one nuance such as sendStatus or choosing correct error classes.
Can set a status in a simple route but lacks HTTP semantics or lifecycle awareness.
Answer would likely produce misleading API responses.

followUps.question: What status would you use for a successful create, a validation error, and a missing resource?
Why is res.json({ error }).status(404) wrong?

en.title: Setting HTTP status codes
en.displayQuestion: How do you set the HTTP status code in an Express response?
en.displayIntent: Check status-setting syntax, ordering, and API status-code judgment.
guidance: Use res.status(code) before sending the response.
Mention res.sendStatus(code) and when it is appropriate.
Give examples for success, validation, auth, not found, conflict, and server errors.
Explain why status cannot reliably be changed after headers are sent.
commonMistakes: Calling status after json or send.
Returning 200 for every error.
Using 500 for client mistakes.
Confusing res.status with res.sendStatus.

vi.title: Set HTTP status code
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để set HTTP status code trong response?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cú pháp set status, thứ tự gọi method và judgment chọn status code cho API.
guidance: Dùng res.status(code) trước khi gửi response.
Nhắc tới res.sendStatus(code) và khi nào phù hợp.
Cho ví dụ cho success, validation, auth, not found, conflict và server error.
Giải thích vì sao không thể đổi status đáng tin cậy sau khi header đã gửi.
commonMistakes: Gọi status sau json hoặc send.
Luôn trả 200 cho mọi lỗi.
Dùng 500 cho lỗi do client gửi sai.
Nhầm res.status với res.sendStatus.

ja.title: HTTP status code を設定する
ja.displayQuestion: Express response で HTTP status code をどう設定しますか。
ja.displayIntent: status 設定の syntax、順序、API status code の判断を確認する。
guidance: response を送る前に res.status(code) を使う。
res.sendStatus(code) と適切な利用場面に触れる。
success、validation、auth、not found、conflict、server error の例を出す。
headers 送信後は status を確実に変更できない理由を説明する。
commonMistakes: json や send の後に status を呼ぶ。
すべての error で 200 を返す。
client mistake に 500 を使う。
res.status と res.sendStatus を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.status()
sourceReferences.note: Used for chainable status setting before sending a response.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.sendStatus()
sourceReferences.note: Used for shortcut status response behavior.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands Express response method chaining and the difference between configuring a response and ending it.
primaryQuestion: Why can you chain res.status(404).json({ ... }) in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that many Express response methods return the response object itself, allowing method chaining.
Specifically states that res.status(code) sets response.statusCode and returns res.
Explains that .json(...) then serializes and sends the response, ending the response cycle.
Understands the order: configuration methods such as status, set, type, or cookie should run before a send/end method.
Recognizes that chaining is syntactic convenience, equivalent to calling res.status(404); res.json(...).
Mentions that after json/send/end is called, the handler should not continue to send another response.

redFlags: Thinks chaining creates a new response object.
Believes every res method is safely chainable after the response is sent.
Cannot explain why status must come before json.
Calls res.json(...).status(404) and expects the status to change.
Continues execution and sends a second response after a chained send.

scoringHints.description: Explains return value, statusCode mutation, final send behavior, ordering, and equivalence to separate statements.
Knows res.status returns res but misses lifecycle or ordering nuances.
Can use chained syntax but cannot explain the underlying mechanics.
Answer suggests unsafe response ordering or duplicate sends.

followUps.question: Rewrite res.status(404).json({ error: 'not found' }) as two separate statements.
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate sees chaining as returned-object convenience.

followUps.question: Why does the order matter between res.status(404).json(...) and res.json(...).status(404)?

en.title: Response method chaining
en.displayQuestion: Why can you chain res.status(404).json({ ... }) in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands Express chainable methods and response-ending order.
guidance: State that res.status() returns the same response object.
Explain that json() sends the response after status has been configured.
Contrast configuration methods with final send methods.
Mention that chaining is only syntax convenience, not a different response lifecycle.
commonMistakes: Thinking chaining creates a new response.
Putting status after json.
Sending a second response after a chained call.
Assuming all response methods can be called after headers are sent.

vi.title: Chain method trên response
vi.displayQuestion: res.status(404).json({...}) — tại sao có thể chain như vậy?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu chainable method trong Express và thứ tự kết thúc response hay không.
guidance: Nói rõ res.status() trả lại chính response object.
Giải thích json() gửi response sau khi status đã được cấu hình.
Phân biệt method cấu hình response với method gửi response cuối cùng.
Nhắc rằng chaining chỉ là tiện ích cú pháp, không tạo vòng đời response khác.
commonMistakes: Tưởng chaining tạo response mới.
Đặt status sau json.
Gửi response lần hai sau một chained call.
Tưởng mọi response method đều gọi được sau khi header đã gửi.

ja.title: response method chaining
ja.displayQuestion: Express で res.status(404).json({ ... }) のように chain できるのはなぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: Express の chainable method と response を終える順序を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: res.status() は同じ response object を返すと述べる。
status 設定後に json() が response を送ると説明する。
response configuration method と final send method を区別する。
chaining は syntax convenience であり lifecycle が変わるわけではないと説明する。
commonMistakes: chaining が新しい response を作ると考える。
json の後に status を置く。
chained call の後に二つ目の response を送る。
headers 送信後もすべての response method を呼べると考える。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.status()
sourceReferences.note: Used for the statement that res.status is chainable.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.json()
sourceReferences.note: Used for JSON response final sending behavior.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands redirect responses, common redirect status codes, and the client/cache/security consequences of choosing permanent versus temporary redirects.
primaryQuestion: What is res.redirect() used for? How are 301 and 302 redirects different?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that res.redirect() sends an HTTP redirect response with a Location header so the client requests another URL.
Mentions that Express defaults to 302 Found when no status is provided.
Explains 301 Moved Permanently as a permanent redirect that clients and search engines may cache and use for canonical URL changes.
Explains 302 Found as a temporary redirect, commonly used when the destination may change, such as login flows or temporary routing.
Mentions related method-preserving redirects such as 307 and 308 when preserving POST/PUT semantics matters.
Warns about open redirect vulnerabilities when redirect targets come from user input.

redFlags: Uses 301 for login redirects or temporary A/B flows without understanding caching consequences.
Cannot explain that redirects are HTTP responses rather than server-side forwarding.
Builds redirects from unvalidated user-controlled returnUrl values.
Assumes all redirect statuses preserve the original HTTP method.
Does not know that Express defaults to 302.

scoringHints.description: Explains Location-based client navigation, default 302, 301 versus 302 cache/intent semantics, 307/308 method preservation, and open redirect risk.
Understands permanent versus temporary redirects but misses default status, method preservation, or security nuance.
Can call res.redirect but cannot choose redirect status based on product and HTTP semantics.
Answer risks incorrect permanent redirects or open redirect vulnerabilities.

followUps.question: Why is 301 usually a bad choice for a temporary login redirect?
How would you prevent an open redirect when using a returnUrl query parameter?

en.title: Express redirects
en.displayQuestion: What is res.redirect() used for? How are 301 and 302 redirects different?
en.displayIntent: Check redirect semantics, status-code choice, and redirect security.
guidance: Explain that redirects send a Location response for the client to follow.
Mention Express default 302 behavior.
Compare 301 permanent redirects with 302 temporary redirects.
Discuss caching, SEO, method preservation with 307/308, and open redirect risk.
commonMistakes: Using 301 for temporary flows.
Treating redirect as server-side routing only.
Trusting user-provided redirect URLs.
Ignoring method changes across redirect statuses.

vi.title: Redirect trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: res.redirect() dùng để làm gì? Các loại redirect (301, 302) khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về redirect, cách chọn status code và bảo mật redirect.
guidance: Giải thích redirect gửi response có Location để client đi tới URL khác.
Nhắc rằng Express mặc định dùng 302 nếu không truyền status.
So sánh 301 permanent redirect với 302 temporary redirect.
Nói về caching, SEO, method preservation với 307/308 và rủi ro open redirect.
commonMistakes: Dùng 301 cho flow tạm thời.
Xem redirect chỉ là routing nội bộ server.
Tin URL redirect do user truyền vào.
Bỏ qua việc HTTP method có thể thay đổi tùy status redirect.

ja.title: Express redirect
ja.displayQuestion: res.redirect() は何に使い、301 と 302 redirect はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: redirect semantics、status code の選択、redirect security を確認する。
guidance: redirect は client が追従する Location response を返すと説明する。
Express の default が 302 である点に触れる。
301 permanent redirect と 302 temporary redirect を比較する。
caching、SEO、307/308 による method preservation、open redirect risk を説明する。
commonMistakes: temporary flow に 301 を使う。
redirect を server-side routing だけとして扱う。
user-provided redirect URL を信頼する。
redirect status による method change を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.redirect()
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express redirect syntax and default 302 behavior.

sourceReferences.label: MDN: 301 Moved Permanently
sourceReferences.note: Used for permanent redirect semantics.

sourceReferences.label: MDN: 302 Found
sourceReferences.note: Used for temporary redirect semantics.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands setting and clearing cookies in Express, cookie attributes, and common security requirements.
primaryQuestion: What are res.cookie() and res.clearCookie() used for in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that res.cookie(name, value, options) sets a Set-Cookie response header for the client to store a cookie.
Explains that res.clearCookie(name, options) instructs the client to remove a cookie by sending an expired Set-Cookie header.
Mentions important cookie options such as httpOnly, secure, sameSite, maxAge, expires, path, domain, signed, and priority where relevant.
Understands that clearing a cookie usually requires matching key options such as path and domain used when setting it.
Distinguishes setting cookies from reading cookies, which requires parsing request Cookie headers, commonly through cookie-parser or similar middleware.
Avoids storing sensitive secrets directly in client-visible cookies and uses httpOnly/secure/sameSite according to the auth model.

redFlags: Thinks res.cookie stores data on the server.
Thinks res.clearCookie can delete any cookie regardless of path or domain.
Stores passwords, raw access tokens, or sensitive profile data in plain cookies.
Forgets httpOnly, secure, and sameSite for authentication-related cookies.
Confuses signed cookies with encrypted cookies.

scoringHints.description: Explains Set-Cookie behavior, clear-cookie matching, security flags, signed versus encrypted meaning, and read/write distinction.
Understands setting and clearing cookies but misses one security or matching-options nuance.
Can call the methods but cannot reason about browser cookie behavior or security attributes.
Answer could create insecure or non-clearable cookies.

followUps.question: If you set a cookie with path '/admin', what option should you remember when clearing it?
What do httpOnly, secure, and sameSite reduce in an auth cookie setup?

en.title: Setting and clearing cookies
en.displayQuestion: What are res.cookie() and res.clearCookie() used for in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check cookie write, clear, and security fundamentals in Express responses.
guidance: Explain Set-Cookie response headers and browser storage.
Describe how clearCookie expires a cookie and why path/domain options matter.
Mention security attributes for auth-related cookies.
Separate writing cookies from reading cookies on req.
commonMistakes: Thinking cookies are stored server-side by res.cookie.
Forgetting matching options when clearing cookies.
Confusing signed cookies with encrypted cookies.
Putting sensitive data directly into client-visible cookies.

vi.title: Set và clear cookie
vi.displayQuestion: res.cookie() và res.clearCookie() dùng để làm gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng ghi cookie, xóa cookie và bảo mật cookie trong Express response.
guidance: Giải thích Set-Cookie response header và việc browser lưu cookie.
Mô tả clearCookie làm cookie hết hạn và vì sao path/domain option quan trọng.
Nhắc các attribute bảo mật cho cookie liên quan auth.
Tách việc ghi cookie với việc đọc cookie trên req.
commonMistakes: Tưởng res.cookie lưu dữ liệu phía server.
Quên option tương ứng khi clear cookie.
Nhầm signed cookie với encrypted cookie.
Đưa dữ liệu nhạy cảm trực tiếp vào cookie client nhìn thấy được.

ja.title: cookie の設定と削除
ja.displayQuestion: Express の res.cookie() と res.clearCookie() は何に使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express response における cookie の書き込み、削除、security の基礎を確認する。
guidance: Set-Cookie response header と browser storage を説明する。
clearCookie が cookie を expire させること、path/domain option が重要な理由を説明する。
auth cookie に関わる security attributes に触れる。
cookie の書き込みと req での読み取りを区別する。
commonMistakes: res.cookie が server-side に保存すると考える。
clear cookie 時に matching options を忘れる。
signed cookie と encrypted cookie を混同する。
client-visible cookie に sensitive data を直接入れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.cookie()
sourceReferences.note: Used for cookie setting options and signed cookie support.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.clearCookie()
sourceReferences.note: Used for clear-cookie behavior and matching option requirements.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands request IP and method metadata, including proxy effects and operational use cases.
primaryQuestion: What information do req.ip and req.method provide in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that req.ip contains the remote IP address Express associates with the request.
Mentions that req.ip changes behind proxies depending on the trust proxy setting and X-Forwarded-For handling.
Explains that req.method contains the HTTP method string such as GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, or OPTIONS.
Connects req.method to route matching, logging, metrics, authorization rules, CORS preflight, and method-specific behavior.
Warns that req.ip should be used carefully for rate limiting or auditing when proxy trust is misconfigured.
Mentions that IP is not a reliable user identity and can represent NAT, proxies, mobile networks, or load balancers.

redFlags: Treats req.ip as a guaranteed unique user identifier.
Ignores trust proxy and blindly trusts X-Forwarded-For from the public internet.
Thinks req.method is the route path or controller name.
Uses req.method for security decisions without route and authorization context.
Cannot explain why req.ip may be a load balancer IP in production.

scoringHints.description: Explains req.ip, req.method, proxy/trust behavior, operational use cases, and limitations for identity/security.
Defines both properties but misses proxy behavior or security implications.
Knows the property names but cannot reason about production deployment behavior.
Answer would lead to unreliable logging, rate limiting, or security decisions.

followUps.question: Why might req.ip be the load balancer address instead of the user's real address?
Where would req.method show up in useful API telemetry?
followUps.purpose: Probe logging and monitoring use cases.

en.title: Request IP and method
en.displayQuestion: What information do req.ip and req.method provide in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check request metadata understanding, especially behind proxies.
guidance: Define req.ip as the remote address Express derives for the request.
Explain trust proxy and X-Forwarded-For implications.
Define req.method as the HTTP method string.
Connect both fields to logging, metrics, routing, authorization, and rate limiting.
commonMistakes: Treating IP as a stable user identity.
Ignoring proxy configuration.
Confusing method with path.
Building rate limits on spoofable IP data.

vi.title: Request IP và method
vi.displayQuestion: req.ip và req.method cho biết thông tin gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về metadata của request, đặc biệt khi chạy sau proxy.
guidance: Định nghĩa req.ip là remote address mà Express suy ra cho request.
Giải thích ảnh hưởng của trust proxy và X-Forwarded-For.
Định nghĩa req.method là chuỗi HTTP method.
Liên hệ hai trường này với logging, metric, routing, authorization và rate limiting.
commonMistakes: Xem IP là định danh user ổn định.
Bỏ qua cấu hình proxy.
Nhầm method với path.
Dựng rate limit trên IP data có thể bị spoof.

ja.title: request IP と method
ja.displayQuestion: Express の req.ip と req.method はどのような情報を持ちますか。
ja.displayIntent: 特に proxy 配下での request metadata の理解を確認する。
guidance: req.ip は Express が request に対して導出する remote address だと定義する。
trust proxy と X-Forwarded-For の影響を説明する。
req.method は HTTP method string だと定義する。
logging、metrics、routing、authorization、rate limiting と結びつける。
commonMistakes: IP を安定した user identity として扱う。
proxy configuration を無視する。
method と path を混同する。
spoof 可能な IP data で rate limit を構築する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.ip
sourceReferences.note: Used for remote IP behavior and trust proxy impact.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.method
sourceReferences.note: Used for HTTP method property behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Behind proxies
sourceReferences.note: Used for proxy-aware IP handling context.

intent: Verify whether the candidate knows how to set response headers and understands timing, overwrite behavior, and common header use cases.
primaryQuestion: How do you set response headers in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Uses res.set('Header-Name', 'value') or res.header('Header-Name', 'value') to set a response header.
Mentions passing an object to res.set() to set multiple headers at once.
Mentions res.append() when adding values to an existing header is desired, and understands res.set() can overwrite/reset header values.
Sets headers before sending the response body or ending the response.
Gives examples such as Cache-Control, Content-Type, ETag, Location, Content-Disposition, security headers, or custom request-id headers.
Avoids manually setting hop-by-hop or invalid headers without understanding HTTP semantics.

redFlags: Uses req.headers to set response headers.
Sets headers after res.send() or res.json() has already committed the response.
Confuses request header reading with response header writing.
Overwrites Set-Cookie or multi-value headers unintentionally.
Adds security headers blindly without understanding their effect.

scoringHints.description: Explains res.set/header, multiple headers, append versus overwrite, timing before send, and practical header examples.
Can set headers correctly but misses append/overwrite behavior or response lifecycle timing.
Knows there is a method but confuses direction or sends headers at the wrong time.
Answer would likely produce missing, overwritten, or late headers.

followUps.question: How would you set Cache-Control and X-Request-Id in the same response?
What happens if you try to set a header after res.json() has already sent the response?

en.title: Setting response headers
en.displayQuestion: How do you set response headers in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check response header writing syntax, timing, and overwrite behavior.
guidance: Use res.set() or res.header() for response headers.
Mention setting multiple headers with an object.
Explain append versus set when multi-value headers matter.
State that headers must be set before the response is sent.
commonMistakes: Using req.headers to set response headers.
Setting headers after sending the body.
Overwriting multi-value headers unintentionally.
Mixing up response headers and request headers.

vi.title: Set response header
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để set response headers trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cú pháp ghi response header, thời điểm set và hành vi overwrite.
guidance: Dùng res.set() hoặc res.header() cho response header.
Nhắc việc set nhiều header bằng một object.
Giải thích append khác set khi header có nhiều value.
Nói rõ header phải set trước khi response được gửi.
commonMistakes: Dùng req.headers để set response header.
Set header sau khi đã gửi body.
Vô tình overwrite header có nhiều value.
Nhầm response header với request header.

ja.title: response header を設定する
ja.displayQuestion: Express で response headers をどう設定しますか。
ja.displayIntent: response header の書き込み syntax、timing、overwrite behavior を確認する。
guidance: response header には res.set() または res.header() を使う。
object で複数 header を設定できる点に触れる。
multi-value header では append と set の違いを説明する。
response 送信前に header を設定する必要があると述べる。
commonMistakes: response header 設定に req.headers を使う。
body 送信後に header を設定する。
multi-value header を意図せず overwrite する。
response header と request header を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.set()
sourceReferences.note: Used for setting one or multiple response headers.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.append()
sourceReferences.note: Used for append versus set behavior.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands per-request response locals, template rendering use cases, and safe middleware-to-view data passing.
primaryQuestion: What is res.locals, and what is it used for?

expectedSignals.label: Defines res.locals as an object for variables scoped to a single request-response cycle.
Explains that values on res.locals are accessible to templates rendered with res.render().
Uses it for request-level data such as authenticated user, permissions, flash messages, request id, locale, CSRF token, or feature flags.
Distinguishes res.locals from app.locals, which is application-wide and shared across requests.
Mentions that res.locals should not be used as persistent storage or cross-request state.
Warns against putting unsanitized user-controlled keys or sensitive data into template locals in a way that can cause XSS or data exposure.

redFlags: Thinks res.locals persists globally across all users.
Uses res.locals as a database or cache.
Cannot distinguish res.locals from app.locals.
Stores large sensitive objects or secrets in locals without considering rendering exposure.
Does not know its main connection to server-side templates.

scoringHints.description: Explains per-request scope, template accessibility, middleware data passing, app.locals distinction, and security boundaries.
Understands request-scoped locals for templates but misses app.locals or security nuance.
Has seen res.locals but cannot explain lifecycle or practical use cases.
Answer confuses request-scoped and application-scoped state.

followUps.question: How would auth middleware expose the current user to later templates without using a global variable?
Why is res.locals safer for request data than app.locals, but still not a place for secrets you render accidentally?

en.title: res.locals request-scoped data
en.displayQuestion: What is res.locals, and what is it used for?
en.displayIntent: Check request-scoped locals and server-side rendering data flow.
guidance: Define res.locals as per-request response-local variables.
Connect it to res.render() and template variables.
Give examples like user, locale, request id, CSRF token, or flash messages.
Distinguish it from app.locals and mention exposure risks.
commonMistakes: Treating res.locals as global state.
Confusing it with app.locals.
Using it as persistent storage.
Rendering sensitive or unsafe data accidentally.

vi.title: Dữ liệu request-scoped trong res.locals
vi.displayQuestion: res.locals là gì và dùng để làm gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về request-scoped locals và luồng truyền dữ liệu tới server-side rendering.
guidance: Định nghĩa res.locals là các biến local của response trong phạm vi một request.
Liên hệ với res.render() và biến trong template.
Cho ví dụ như user, locale, request id, CSRF token hoặc flash message.
Phân biệt với app.locals và nhắc rủi ro lộ dữ liệu khi render.
commonMistakes: Xem res.locals là global state.
Nhầm với app.locals.
Dùng nó như persistent storage.
Vô tình render dữ liệu nhạy cảm hoặc không an toàn.

ja.title: res.locals の request-scoped data
ja.displayQuestion: res.locals とは何で、何に使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: request-scoped locals と server-side rendering への data flow を確認する。
guidance: res.locals は request ごとの response-local variables だと定義する。
res.render() と template variables に結びつける。
user、locale、request id、CSRF token、flash message などの例を出す。
app.locals と区別し、data exposure risk に触れる。
commonMistakes: res.locals を global state として扱う。
app.locals と混同する。
persistent storage として使う。
sensitive または unsafe data を誤って render する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.locals
sourceReferences.note: Used for per-request local variables and template rendering access.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: app.locals
sourceReferences.note: Used for contrasting application-wide template locals.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands URL-related request properties in Express and how mounted routers/middleware rewrite req.url.
primaryQuestion: How are req.path, req.url, and req.originalUrl different in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that req.path is the path portion of the current request URL without the query string.
Explains that req.url is inherited from Node.js and represents the current URL used by the active middleware/router, including the query string.
Explains that Express can rewrite req.url when middleware or routers are mounted, commonly stripping the mount point.
Explains that req.originalUrl preserves the original requested URL before internal routing rewrites and is available in middleware and routers.
Mentions req.baseUrl as the mounted path prefix that helps explain originalUrl as baseUrl plus url in router contexts.
Uses an example such as app.use('/admin', ...) receiving /admin/new?sort=desc where originalUrl is /admin/new?sort=desc, baseUrl is /admin, path is /new, and url is /new?sort=desc.

redFlags: Says req.path, req.url, and req.originalUrl are always identical.
Ignores query string differences.
Cannot explain why mounted routers may see a shortened req.url.
Uses req.url for audit logging when originalUrl is needed.
Uses string splitting instead of URL-aware properties when Express already provides them.

scoringHints.description: Correctly explains path, current url, originalUrl, query string differences, mounted router rewriting, and baseUrl with a concrete example.
Understands most differences but misses mounted-router behavior or baseUrl.
Only knows that these properties relate to URLs but cannot choose reliably.
Answer would lead to incorrect logging, redirects, or middleware matching.

followUps.question: Inside middleware mounted at /api, what might req.url and req.originalUrl look like for /api/users?page=1?
Which property would you log if you need the exact URL the client requested?

en.title: req.path, req.url, and req.originalUrl
en.displayQuestion: How are req.path, req.url, and req.originalUrl different in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check URL property understanding, especially in mounted routers and middleware.
guidance: Define req.path as the path portion without query string.
Define req.url as the current internal URL, including query string.
Define req.originalUrl as the original client URL preserved before routing rewrites.
Use a mounted-router example and mention req.baseUrl.
commonMistakes: Saying all three are the same.
Forgetting query string differences.
Ignoring mounted router URL rewriting.
Logging req.url when originalUrl is required.

vi.title: req.path, req.url và req.originalUrl
vi.displayQuestion: req.path và req.url và req.originalUrl khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về các URL property, đặc biệt trong router và middleware được mount.
guidance: Định nghĩa req.path là phần path không gồm query string.
Định nghĩa req.url là URL nội bộ hiện tại, có thể gồm query string.
Định nghĩa req.originalUrl là URL client gọi ban đầu, được giữ lại trước các rewrite nội bộ.
Dùng ví dụ mounted router và nhắc tới req.baseUrl.
commonMistakes: Nói cả ba luôn giống nhau.
Quên khác biệt về query string.
Bỏ qua việc mounted router rewrite URL.
Log req.url khi cần originalUrl.

ja.title: req.path、req.url、req.originalUrl
ja.displayQuestion: Express の req.path、req.url、req.originalUrl はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: 特に mounted routers と middleware における URL property の理解を確認する。
guidance: req.path は query string を含まない path portion だと定義する。
req.url は query string を含み得る current internal URL だと定義する。
req.originalUrl は routing rewrite 前の original client URL だと定義する。
mounted-router example を使い、req.baseUrl に触れる。
commonMistakes: 3 つが同じだと言う。
query string difference を忘れる。
mounted router の URL rewriting を無視する。
originalUrl が必要な場面で req.url を log する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.path
sourceReferences.note: Used for path portion behavior and mounted middleware note.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.originalUrl
sourceReferences.note: Used for original URL preservation and baseUrl/url example.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js HTTP API: message.url
sourceReferences.note: Used for the underlying req.url inherited from Node.js.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can implement file downloads safely using Express helpers while handling paths, authorization, errors, and partially-sent responses.
primaryQuestion: How do you download a file in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Uses res.download(path[, filename][, options][, callback]) when the desired behavior is attachment-style file download.
Mentions res.sendFile() as an alternative for sending a file inline or with custom headers when download semantics are not required.
Validates authorization before sending the file and avoids exposing arbitrary filesystem paths from user input.
Uses safe path construction, allowlists, storage IDs, or root options instead of concatenating untrusted path segments directly.
Handles callback errors and knows the response may be partially sent, so error handlers should check res.headersSent.
Sets or understands headers such as Content-Disposition, Content-Type, Cache-Control, and filename where relevant.
Considers streaming, large files, CDN/object storage signed URLs, and range requests for production-scale downloads.

redFlags: Passes req.query.path directly to res.download.
Performs authorization after starting the download.
Ignores callback errors or tries to send a JSON error after a partial file response.
Serves private files from a static public directory.
Loads large files fully into memory before sending without a reason.

scoringHints.description: Explains res.download usage, safe path handling, auth, headers, callback/partial response handling, and production alternatives.
Can use res.download safely for basic cases but misses one production concern such as partial errors or storage strategy.
Can send a file but does not reason about traversal, authorization, or error handling.
Answer risks filesystem exposure or broken partial-download behavior.

followUps.question: Why is res.download(req.query.path) dangerous?
followUps.purpose: Check path traversal and authorization risk awareness.

followUps.question: What should your callback do if an error occurs after headers have already been sent?

en.title: File downloads in Express
en.displayQuestion: How do you download a file in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check safe file download implementation with Express response helpers.
guidance: Use res.download() for attachment-style downloads.
Discuss res.sendFile() when inline file serving is more appropriate.
Validate authorization and construct paths safely.
Handle callback errors and partial response cases.
Mention headers, large file strategy, and object storage/CDN alternatives.
commonMistakes: Using user input directly as a filesystem path.
Skipping authorization before download.
Sending a second response after a partial file send.
Loading large files into memory unnecessarily.

vi.title: Download file trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để download file trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách implement download file an toàn bằng response helper của Express.
guidance: Dùng res.download() cho download dạng attachment.
Nói về res.sendFile() khi cần serve file inline.
Validate authorization và build path an toàn.
Xử lý callback error và trường hợp response đã gửi một phần.
Nhắc tới header, chiến lược file lớn và lựa chọn object storage/CDN.
commonMistakes: Dùng trực tiếp user input làm filesystem path.
Bỏ qua authorization trước khi download.
Gửi response thứ hai sau khi file đã gửi một phần.
Load file lớn vào memory không cần thiết.

ja.title: Express で file download
ja.displayQuestion: Express で file を download させるにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express response helper を使った安全な file download 実装を確認する。
guidance: attachment-style download には res.download() を使う。
inline file serving では res.sendFile() が適切な場合もあると説明する。
authorization を検証し、path を安全に組み立てる。
callback error と partial response case を扱う。
headers、大きな file の戦略、object storage/CDN alternatives に触れる。
commonMistakes: user input をそのまま filesystem path として使う。
download 前の authorization を省略する。
file が部分的に送られた後に二つ目の response を送る。
大きな file を不要に memory へ読み込む。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.download()
sourceReferences.note: Used for file download syntax, callback behavior, and partial response warning.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.sendFile()
sourceReferences.note: Used for direct file sending and safe root option context.

intent: Check whether the candidate can serve static assets with express.static while considering mount paths, filesystem roots, caching, fallthrough, and exposure risks.
primaryQuestion: How do you serve static files in Express?

expectedSignals.label: Uses express.static(root, options) as built-in middleware and mounts it with app.use().
Explains that the root directory is the filesystem directory Express serves from, and that the directory name itself is not part of the URL unless mounted under a virtual prefix.
Shows a virtual mount path such as app.use('/static', express.static(path.join(__dirname, 'public'))).
Prefers absolute paths or path.join with a known application directory to avoid surprises from process working directory changes.
Configures caching carefully, such as long maxAge and immutable only for versioned assets, and shorter/no cache for mutable files.
Considers dotfiles, directory exposure, fallthrough behavior, SPA fallbacks, and custom 404 handling.
Mentions that reverse proxy, CDN, or object storage is often better for high-volume static assets in production.

redFlags: Serves the whole project root or private upload directory without review.
Assumes express.static performs authorization for private files.
Caches mutable non-versioned files for a long time.
Uses relative paths without understanding the process working directory.
Relies on Express alone for heavy static traffic where a CDN or reverse proxy should be considered.

scoringHints.description: Covers express.static syntax, mount paths, absolute roots, cache strategy, dotfiles/fallthrough, SPA fallback, and production offload.
Can serve a public directory but misses one safety or production concern.
Knows app.use(express.static(...)) but cannot reason about URL mapping, cache, or exposure risk.
Answer may expose private files or create stale asset behavior.

followUps.question: Which assets would you serve with immutable long-term cache headers, and which would you avoid caching that way?
What can go wrong if you serve your repository root as static content?

en.title: Serving static files
en.displayQuestion: How do you serve static files in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check express.static usage with safe paths, caching, and production considerations.
guidance: Use express.static(root, options) mounted with app.use().
Explain filesystem root versus URL mount path.
Prefer known absolute paths over fragile relative paths.
Discuss cache headers, dotfiles, fallthrough, SPA fallback, and CDN/reverse proxy offload.
commonMistakes: Serving too broad a filesystem directory.
Treating static middleware as authorization.
Using long cache headers for mutable files.
Ignoring production offload for heavy static traffic.

vi.title: Serve static file
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để serve static files trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách dùng express.static với path an toàn, caching và cân nhắc production.
guidance: Dùng express.static(root, options) và mount bằng app.use().
Giải thích filesystem root khác URL mount path.
Ưu tiên absolute path đã biết thay vì relative path dễ phụ thuộc working directory.
Nói về cache header, dotfile, fallthrough, SPA fallback và offload sang CDN/reverse proxy.
commonMistakes: Serve một thư mục filesystem quá rộng.
Xem static middleware như authorization.
Dùng cache header dài cho file có thể thay đổi.
Bỏ qua offload production khi static traffic lớn.

ja.title: static files を配信する
ja.displayQuestion: Express で static files をどう serve しますか。
ja.displayIntent: 安全な path、caching、production consideration を含めて express.static usage を確認する。
guidance: app.use() で mount した express.static(root, options) を使う。
filesystem root と URL mount path の違いを説明する。
fragile な relative path より既知の absolute path を優先する。
cache headers、dotfiles、fallthrough、SPA fallback、CDN/reverse proxy offload に触れる。
commonMistakes: 広すぎる filesystem directory を配信する。
static middleware を authorization として扱う。
mutable files に長い cache headers を使う。
static traffic が多い production で offload を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Serving static files
sourceReferences.note: Used for express.static usage, mount paths, and absolute path guidance.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: express.static()
sourceReferences.note: Used for static middleware options such as cache, dotfiles, and fallthrough.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands server-side template rendering in Express, required configuration, and when rendering HTML differs from returning API JSON.
primaryQuestion: What is res.render() used for? What Express configuration is needed to use it?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that res.render(view, locals[, callback]) renders a server-side template and sends the resulting HTML response unless a callback is used.
Mentions configuring a view engine with app.set('view engine', 'pug' or 'ejs' or another engine).
Mentions configuring the views directory with app.set('views', pathToViews) when the default views directory is not sufficient.
Mentions installing the corresponding template engine package, such as pug, ejs, handlebars, or another Express-compatible engine.
Explains passing locals to the template through the second argument and/or res.locals for request-scoped values.
Distinguishes res.render() for HTML pages from res.json() for API responses.
Mentions callback behavior: with a callback, Express provides the rendered HTML and the handler must send it explicitly.

redFlags: Uses res.render() expecting JSON output for an API endpoint.
Forgets to configure view engine or install the template engine package.
Cannot explain where Express looks for templates.
Puts unsanitized user input into templates without considering escaping and XSS.
Provides a callback to res.render() but forgets to send the rendered HTML.

scoringHints.description: Explains rendering behavior, view engine setup, views path, package installation, locals/res.locals, callback behavior, and HTML versus JSON distinction.
Understands res.render and view engine setup but misses callback, locals, or security nuance.
Has used templates but cannot describe the required Express configuration.
Answer confuses server-side rendering with API serialization or static file serving.

followUps.question: What happens if you call res.render('index') without setting a view engine or using a filename extension?
How would you pass the current user and page title into a rendered template?

en.title: Server-side rendering with res.render
en.displayQuestion: What is res.render() used for? What Express configuration is needed to use it?
en.displayIntent: Check server-side template rendering setup and behavior in Express.
guidance: Define res.render() as rendering a template into HTML.
Mention app.set('view engine', ...) and app.set('views', ...).
Mention installing the matching template engine package.
Explain locals, res.locals, callback behavior, and the difference from JSON API responses.
commonMistakes: Using res.render for JSON APIs.
Forgetting view engine setup or package installation.
Not knowing where templates are loaded from.
Forgetting to send HTML when using a render callback.

vi.title: Server-side rendering với res.render
vi.displayQuestion: res.render() dùng để làm gì? Express cần cấu hình gì để dùng được nó?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách cấu hình và hành vi server-side template rendering trong Express.
guidance: Định nghĩa res.render() là render template thành HTML.
Nhắc app.set('view engine', ...) và app.set('views', ...).
Nhắc cài package template engine tương ứng.
Giải thích locals, res.locals, callback behavior và khác biệt với JSON API response.
commonMistakes: Dùng res.render cho JSON API.
Quên cấu hình view engine hoặc cài package.
Không biết template được load từ đâu.
Quên gửi HTML khi dùng callback của render.

ja.title: res.render による server-side rendering
ja.displayQuestion: res.render() は何に使い、使うために Express で何を設定する必要がありますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express における server-side template rendering の設定と behavior を確認する。
guidance: res.render() は template を HTML に render すると定義する。
app.set('view engine', ...) と app.set('views', ...) に触れる。
対応する template engine package の install に触れる。
locals、res.locals、callback behavior、JSON API response との違いを説明する。
commonMistakes: JSON API に res.render を使う。
view engine setup や package installation を忘れる。
template がどこから load されるか分からない。
render callback 使用時に HTML を送信し忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.render()
sourceReferences.note: Used for render syntax, locals, callback behavior, and automatic response behavior.
